zones.name: Welcome Hub
zones.tagline: Your gateway to understanding the world's largest democracy
zones.description: Start your journey through the Indian election process. Learn what elections are about, why your vote matters, and how the democratic system works in India.
zones.assistant_intro: Welcome! I'm your ELITE Election Guide. I'm here to help you understand every step of the voting process in India. Ask me anything about the Lok Sabha, Vidhan Sabha, registration, or voting!
faq: What elections are coming up?
Why should I vote?
How does the electoral process work in India?
What is the difference between Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha?
key_facts: India is the world's largest democracy with over 960 million eligible voters.
General Elections (Lok Sabha) are held every five years.
The Election Commission of India (ECI) is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes in India.
India uses Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) along with Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail (VVPAT) for elections.

zones.name: Voter Registration
zones.description: Learn about voter registration requirements, eligibility, and how to register in your constituency. We'll guide you through getting your Voter ID.
zones.assistant_intro: Let's make sure you're registered to vote! Getting your Voter ID (EPIC) is the first and most important step. I can help you understand the requirements.
faq: Am I eligible to vote in India?
How do I register to vote?
What documents do I need for a Voter ID?
Can I register online via the NVSP portal?
What is the Form 6 used for?
key_facts: You must be an Indian citizen and 18 years of age on the qualifying date (usually Jan 1, April 1, July 1, or Oct 1 of the year) to vote.
You must be ordinarily resident in the polling area where you want to be enrolled.
You can register online through the Voter Helpline App or the ECI's voter portal.
Form 6 is the application form for new voters.
You cannot be enrolled in more than one place.

zones.name: Election Timeline
zones.description: Indian elections are massive and often happen in multiple phases. Stay updated on the polling dates for your specific state and constituency.
zones.assistant_intro: Indian General Elections happen in phases to ensure security and smooth voting. I can help you find out which phase your constituency falls under.
faq: Why are Indian elections held in phases?
When is the polling date for my state?
What is the Model Code of Conduct?
When is the vote counting day?
key_facts: The 2024 Lok Sabha elections were held in 7 phases from April 19 to June 1.
The Model Code of Conduct comes into effect as soon as the election dates are announced by the ECI.
All votes from all phases are counted on a single designated day.
Campaigning must stop 48 hours before the end of polling in a constituency.

zones.name: Polling Day
zones.description: Everything you need to know about Election Day — from your Voter Slip, to what to expect at the polling booth, to using the EVM.
zones.assistant_intro: It's the big day! I'll walk you through exactly what to expect when you arrive at your polling booth. You'll press the button on the EVM and see the VVPAT slip.
faq: Do I need my Voter ID (EPIC) to vote?
What is an EVM and VVPAT?
Where is my polling booth?
What is the NOTA option?
What do I do if my name is not on the voter list?
key_facts: You can vote if your name is on the electoral roll, even if you don't have your EPIC, by showing other approved ID proofs like Aadhaar or PAN card.
EVM stands for Electronic Voting Machine, and VVPAT is the paper slip that verifies your vote.
NOTA stands for 'None of the Above', allowing you to reject all candidates.
Polling stations are usually set up so that no voter has to travel more than 2 km to cast their vote.

zones.name: Results & Beyond
zones.description: Learn how votes are counted by the ECI, results are declared, and how the government is formed in the Lok Sabha.
zones.assistant_intro: The polls have closed! Let me explain how the EVMs are secured, how the counting happens on Counting Day, and how a majority is formed.
faq: How are EVM votes counted?
When are results declared?
What is the magic number for a majority in the Lok Sabha?
How is the Prime Minister chosen?
What happens if there is a hung parliament?
key_facts: All votes from all phases are counted simultaneously across the country on a single day.
There are 543 elected seats in the Lok Sabha.
A party or coalition needs 272 seats to form a majority government.
The President of India invites the leader of the majority party or coalition to form the government as Prime Minister.
